events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a brake service with two defects:
1. Left rotor measures 23.4 mm, stamped minimum is 24.0 mm - this is below spec, non-compliant/installed-defective
2. Caliper hose has a half twist between hard line and caliper - this is an installation defect that could restrict flow or cause hose failure

The pedal feels firm and pads are new, but the rotor is below minimum thickness and the hose is twisted. Both are defects that matter.

Decision: fail (non-compliant - rotor below minimum thickness is a safety issue)
Risk: high
Work stage: tested/inspected
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 85% - the brake job was done but has defects
Value: 23.4 (the rotor thickness reading)
Confidence: high, maybe 0.95
Findings: rotor below minimum thickness, hose twisted
Actions: replace rotor, replace/re-route hose
Rationale: rotor below stamped minimum is non-compliant and unsafe; twisted hose is installation defect
References: OEM specs (stamped minimum thickness)

    "Caliper hose has a half twist between hard line and caliper with wheels straight — hose is installed-defective, risking flow restriction or premature failure"

    "Replace left rotor with a unit at or above the 24.0 mm minimum thickness specification",
    "Remove twist from caliper hose; verify proper routing and clocking with wheels straight and through full steering travel; replace hose if kinked or damaged"
  ],
  "rationale": "A rotor machined or worn below its stamped minimum thickness cannot dissipate heat properly and is unsafe; a twisted brake hose can restrict fluid return causing drag or lock-up. Both defects are installation/quality issues discovered at inspection, so the repair fails QC despite a firm pedal.",

    "OEM stamped rotor minimum thickness specification (24.0 mm)",
    "OEM brake hose routing and installation procedure"
  ]
